title: Route-consistency conservative loss

queries: graph prompt alignment route consistency schema path signature llm
graph retrieval alignment route-aware conservative supervision relation path consistency

papers.title: Can we Soft Prompt LLMs for Graph Learning Tasks?
papers.claim: Graph-text modality mismatch should be addressed at the alignment interface, where structural information is projected into prompt tokens.
papers.relevance: Supports adding a prompt-side alignment consistency term keyed to route signatures instead of another coefficient-only scan.

papers.title: Toward Graph-Tokenizing Large Language Models with Reconstructive Graph Instruction Tuning
papers.claim: Explicit graph-aware supervision can improve graph-token alignment, but the signal should match the useful structural invariant instead of forcing one global bottleneck.
papers.relevance: Supports replacing failed graph-global reconstruction pressure with a narrower route-signature consistency objective.

papers.title: Graph Retrieval-Augmented Generation: A Survey
papers.claim: Route-aware graph evidence organization is a first-class design axis, not just a prompt-format detail.
papers.relevance: Supports testing route-signature preservation as a structural alignment mechanism before escalating to sampling-layer changes.

evidence.why_now: EXP060, EXP063, and EXP066 all preserved the same failure pattern: stronger or more local conservative alignment keeps helping user-churn, but user-ltv and item-incoterms still regress. After FK-direction alone proved too narrow to recover cross-task stability, the next paper-backed step inside the same family is to preserve route-signature consistency across sampled evidence paths.

hypothesis: The current alignment path still allows tokens coming from the same sampled schema-route pattern to drift apart across examples, so the LLM sees structurally similar evidence encoded inconsistently. Adding a modest batch-level route-signature consistency penalty on token-query states should preserve readability of repeated evidence routes, keep the classification-side gains from stronger alignment, and be broader than FK-direction without falling back to graph-global reconstruction.
notes: This is a finetune-only route-aware conservative loss candidate; do not use pretrain or --pretrain.
Implementation scope is intentionally isolated: add only one route-signature consistency term on top of the refreshed 512-step screening baseline.

If this broader route-aware loss still repeats the churn-up / ltv-down / incoterms-down pattern, the next step should switch layers toward bridge- and route-aware sampling rather than another near-duplicate loss rerun.
Launched on 2026-05-07 after syncing the updated main.py/model.py route-consistency patch plus generated wrappers and launcher to the remote repo and /tmp.
Completed on 2026-05-07 with global verdict: failed.
user-churn was neutral, while user-ltv and item-incoterms both regressed against the refreshed 512-step strict baselines.
